hypothesis: If adaptive multi-metric diversity constraints informed by NaviDiv are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission discovery, then the generated molecule sets will show higher representation/fragment/scaffold/string diversity (e.g., lower mean pairwise Tanimoto, more clusters, higher unique-fragment percentages) without reducing the number of molecules meeting the ΔE_SF criterion compared to unconstrained runs, because dynamically penalizing overrepresented structures prevents mode collapse while preserving objective optimization.
